tossups.question: A text on this specific discipline written by Ji Cheng details the technique of “borrowing” from different angles. After collaborating with Lawrence Halprin on El Novillero, Thomas Church wrote a 1955 book on this discipline outlining the four principles of unity, function, simplicity, and scale. Gertrude Jekyll’s work in this discipline led to her collaboration with Edwin Lutyens on her home of Munstead Wood. This discipline was practiced by Marian Cruger Coffin and Edith Wharton’s niece, Beatrix Farrand. The (*) Emerald Necklace was designed by a practitioner of this discipline who studied under its first American exponent, Andrew Jackson Downing. André Le Nôtre, who worked in this discipline, planned the parterres and hedge mazes of Versailles. For 10 points, Frederick Law Olmsted worked in what discipline that might involve designing Central Park?
tossups.answer: landscape architecture [or landscaping; accept landscape design; accept garden design; accept garden planning; prompt on gardening; prompt on horticulture; prompt on design; prompt on architecture; prompt on urban planning on the “Emerald Necklace” sentence]
tossups.answer_sanitized: landscape architecture [or landscaping; accept landscape design; accept garden design; accept garden planning; prompt on gardening; prompt on horticulture; prompt on design; prompt on architecture; prompt on urban planning on the "Emerald Necklace" sentence]
